title: veronica austriaca subsp. virescens
intro: Veronica austriaca subsp. virescens, also known as the green alpine speedwell, is a plant species in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Veronica austriaca subsp. virescens include its ability to help lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from respiratory issues to digestive problems.
